analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-289

POL-289.title: IMPLEMENT: Foreman/Worker boundary hardening from POL-283 postmortem
POL-289.body:
## Objective

Implement the recommendations from the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) postmortem analysis ([POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary)). Address Foreman/Worker behavioral drift, runtime boundary failures, and observability gaps identified during the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) cluster run.

## Context

[POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) completed all four children successfully but exposed six categories of failure:

* Foreman consumed excessive tokens by observing and narrating worker activity (no quiet mode)
* Worker drifted outside intended operational boundaries (no scope enforcement at commit time)
* Runtime state and artifact files were modified directly during the run (no CLI-owned state writes)
* Foreman performed live intervention and repair (prohibited but not enforced)
* Multiple runtime recovery actions required (no automated escalation)
* Governance boundaries treated as advisory (existing enforcement gaps)

Analysis artifact: `smartdocs/raw/pol-288-foreman-worker-drift-postmortem.md`
Source analysis issue: [POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary)

## Goal

Harden the Foreman/Worker execution boundary through a layered approach: instruction fixes first, then CLI-owned enforcement, then architectural isolation.

## Scope

* `.polaris/roles/foreman.md`
* `.polaris/roles/` (new medic.md)
* `.polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md`
* `src/loop/worker.ts`
* `src/loop/worker-packet.ts`
* `src/loop/continue.ts`
* `src/loop/compact-return.ts`
* `src/cli/`
* `src/loop/telemetry` (new events)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Foreman role file explicitly prohibits observing raw worker output and performing live repair
- [ ] polaris-run chain.md has an explicit CHECKPOINT gate that discards worker output except CompactReturn
- [ ] Worker packet schema includes `prohibited_write_paths`
- [ ] Polaris CLI provides `worker complete` and `worker commit` commands
- [ ] Foreman/Worker telemetry captures CompactReturn size, scope fidelity, and recovery count
- [ ] Medic role is defined with authority boundaries and telemetry events
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop src/cli

## Ordering

Execute children in dependency order: instruction fixes first (T1-1, T1-2), then enforcement additions (T2-1, T2-2, T2-3), then architecture changes (T3 series).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement filesystem sandbox for worker containment (OS-level isolation is out of scope)
* Do not change existing provider configuration
* Do not alter the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) deliverables (cognition librarian lifecycle is complete)

POL-288.title: ANALYZE: POL-283 Foreman/Worker behavioral drift and runtime boundary failures

POL-283.title: IMPLEMENT: Complete cognition librarian lifecycle wiring and adaptive coverage policy

POL-294.title: IMPLEMENT: Foreman/Worker observability — CompactReturn size, scope fidelity, and recovery telemetry
POL-294.body:
## Objective

Add telemetry events that measure CompactReturn size, worker scope fidelity (files touched vs. allowed_scope), and per-run recovery action count — making "excessive token burn" and "worker drift" measurable rather than anecdotal.

## Context

The [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) run exposed that Foreman token consumption and worker scope drift could not be measured from existing telemetry (F5). There are no events for CompactReturn size, out-of-scope file writes, or aggregate recovery counts per run. Without these, future analysis has no quantitative baseline.

Source: [POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary) postmortem `smartdocs/raw/pol-288-foreman-worker-drift-postmortem.md`, section 7.
Parent implementation cluster: [POL-289](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-289/implement-foremanworker-boundary-hardening-from-pol-283-postmortem).

## Goal

Add three new telemetry event types emitted by `continue.ts` and the run ledger: `compact-return-received`, `worker-scope-fidelity`, and a recovery count field on run-complete events.

## Scope

* `src/loop/continue.ts`
* `src/loop/ledger.ts`
* `src/loop/compact-return.ts` (may add size helper)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `continue.ts` emits `compact-return-received` event with `size_bytes` (byte length of the CompactReturn JSON) and `child_id`
- [ ] `continue.ts` emits `worker-scope-fidelity` event with `allowed_scope`, `actual_files_touched` (from git diff of the child's commit), and `out_of_scope_files` (files in actual_files_touched but not in allowed_scope)
- [ ] `ledger.ts` includes `recovery_count` (count of `loop-aborted` events for the same `run_id`) in the `cluster-complete` ledger event
- [ ] All three events include `run_id`, `child_id` (where applicable), and `timestamp`
- [ ] New tests verify the events are emitted with correct fields
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

Can run in parallel with T2-1 ([POL-291](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-291/implement-worker-containment-prohibited-write-paths-in-role-file-and)) and T2-2 ([POL-292](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-292/implement-cli-owned-worker-state-writes-add-polaris-worker-complete)). No hard dependency on other T2 issues.

## Non-goals

* Do not add Foreman context-size telemetry (that requires adapter-level output filtering, a T3 concern)
* Do not gate execution on telemetry values in this issue — observability only
* Do not change the CompactReturn schema (size measurement is post-parse)

POL-293.title: IMPLEMENT: CLI-owned worker commits — add `polaris worker commit` with scope validation
POL-293.body:
## Objective

Add `polaris worker commit` as a CLI-owned commit path that validates staged files against `allowed_scope` and rejects commits that include state/artifact paths or files outside the worker's allowed scope.

## Context

In the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) run, workers could stage and commit any file, including runtime artifacts and files outside their packet's `allowed_scope`. The existing `git-custody.ts` manages delivery branch commits but does not validate scope at commit time. CLI ownership of commits is the medium-term enforcement fix for worker scope drift (F2, F3).

Source: [POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary) postmortem `smartdocs/raw/pol-288-foreman-worker-drift-postmortem.md`, sections 5, 6, 8 (T2-2).
Parent implementation cluster: [POL-289](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-289/implement-foremanworker-boundary-hardening-from-pol-283-postmortem).

## Goal

Add `npm run polaris -- worker commit` that reads the current packet's `allowed_scope` and `prohibited_write_paths`, validates the git staging area against these lists, rejects the commit if violations are found, and creates the commit if valid — returning the commit hash.

## Scope

* `src/cli/` (new worker commit subcommand)
* `src/loop/git-custody.ts` (add scope-validation helper)
* `src/loop/worker-packet.ts` (read allowed_scope for validation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run polaris -- worker commit` exists and is registered in the CLI
- [ ] Command reads `allowed_scope` from the active packet (via bootstrap packet path or env var)
- [ ] Command reads `prohibited_write_paths` from the packet
- [ ] Command runs `git diff --cached --name-only` and validates each file against allowed_scope
- [ ] Command rejects the commit if any staged file is in `prohibited_write_paths`
- [ ] Command rejects the commit if any staged file is outside `allowed_scope`
- [ ] Command creates the commit and prints the commit hash to stdout on success
- [ ] Command exits non-zero and emits `worker-commit-rejected` telemetry on violation
- [ ] New test proves scope-violating staged files are rejected

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli src/loop

## Ordering

Depends on T2-1 ([POL-291](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-291/implement-worker-containment-prohibited-write-paths-in-role-file-and) — CLI-owned worker complete) being done first. This is the second T2 issue.

## Non-goals

* Do not enforce that workers must use this command in this issue — add the command first
* Do not change the existing `git-custody.ts` delivery-branch logic
* Do not implement Polaris Linear mutation ownership here

POL-292.title: IMPLEMENT: CLI-owned worker state writes — add `polaris worker complete` command
POL-292.body:
## Objective

Replace direct worker filesystem writes to `current-state.json` with a CLI-owned `polaris worker complete` command that validates the result file and updates state atomically.

## Context

In the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) run, runtime state files were modified directly (F3). Workers can call `writeStateAtomic` directly or write to `current-state.json` via the fs module. There is no runtime mechanism that prevents this. Polaris CLI ownership of state writes is the medium-term enforcement fix.

Source: [POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary) postmortem `smartdocs/raw/pol-288-foreman-worker-drift-postmortem.md`, sections 6, 8 (T2-1).
Parent implementation cluster: [POL-289](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-289/implement-foremanworker-boundary-hardening-from-pol-283-postmortem).
Depends on: T1-2 ([POL-290](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-290/implement-quiet-foreman-mode-role-file-and-polaris-run-chain-hardening)) being done first is recommended but not required.

## Goal

Add `npm run polaris -- worker complete <result-file>` as the canonical path for workers to report completion. This command validates the result file, updates `current-state.json`, and appends the `worker-complete` telemetry event — replacing ad-hoc direct writes.

## Scope

* `src/cli/` (new worker subcommand)
* `src/loop/worker.ts` (update to call CLI instead of direct write where applicable)
* `src/loop/checkpoint.ts` (may add internal validation helper)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run polaris -- worker complete <result-file>` exists and is registered in the CLI
- [ ] Command reads and validates the result file (status, commit field present)
- [ ] Command updates `current-state.json` atomically via `writeStateAtomic`
- [ ] Command appends `worker-complete` telemetry event to the run's JSONL file
- [ ] Command exits non-zero and emits `worker-complete-failed` telemetry if result file is invalid
- [ ] Existing `executeOneChild` flow in `worker.ts` continues to work (backward compatible)
- [ ] New test covers the CLI command validation and state-update path

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli src/loop

## Ordering

Depends on T1-2 ([POL-290](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-290/implement-quiet-foreman-mode-role-file-and-polaris-run-chain-hardening)) conceptually. Can be implemented in parallel. Must be done before T2-2 (CLI-owned commits).

## Non-goals

* Do not remove the existing `executeOneChild` direct-write path in this issue — add the CLI command first
* Do not implement `worker commit` command here (that is T2-2)
* Do not enforce that all workers use this command in this issue — that is a follow-on enforcement step

POL-291.title: IMPLEMENT: Worker containment — prohibited write paths in role file and packet schema
POL-291.body:
## Objective

Prevent workers from directly writing to runtime state and artifact files by adding a `prohibited_write_paths` field to the worker packet schema and adding explicit prohibitions to the worker role file.

## Context

In the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) run, runtime state and artifact files were modified, staged, or repaired during the run (F3). Workers may have written directly to `.taskchain_artifacts/`, `.polaris/clusters/`, or `current-state.json`. The current packet schema has no `prohibited_write_paths` field, and the worker role file does not explicitly prohibit these paths.

Source: [POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary) postmortem `smartdocs/raw/pol-288-foreman-worker-drift-postmortem.md`, sections 5, 6, and 8.
Parent implementation cluster: [POL-289](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-289/implement-foremanworker-boundary-hardening-from-pol-283-postmortem).

## Goal

Add explicit instruction-level containment: workers receive a `prohibited_write_paths` list in their packet, and the worker role file explicitly prohibits writing to runtime artifact paths.

## Scope

* `src/loop/worker-packet.ts`
* `.polaris/roles/worker.md` (create if not exists, or update)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `WorkerPacket` type includes optional `prohibited_write_paths?: string[]` field
- [ ] `compileImplPacket` populates `prohibited_write_paths` with the canonical list: `.taskchain_artifacts/`, `.polaris/clusters/`, `.polaris/runs/`, telemetry JSONL files
- [ ] Worker role file explicitly states: "Workers must not write to `current-state.json`, cluster-state, telemetry JSONL, or ledger directly. State reporting occurs only through CompactReturn and the designated result file."
- [ ] Worker role file explicitly states: "Workers must not modify or stage files outside `allowed_scope` except: the designated result file path and cognition note paths specified in the packet."
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass; new test verifies `prohibited_write_paths` is populated in a compiled packet

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

Can run in parallel with [POL-290](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-290/implement-quiet-foreman-mode-role-file-and-polaris-run-chain-hardening) (T1-1, the Foreman role fix). No dependency on T2 issues.

## Non-goals

* Do not add runtime enforcement of these paths in this issue — instruction-only fix
* Do not implement `worker complete` CLI command here (that is a T2 issue)
* Do not modify `dispatch-boundary.ts` or `continue.ts`

POL-290.title: IMPLEMENT: Quiet Foreman mode — role file and polaris-run chain hardening
POL-290.body:
## Objective

Prevent the Foreman from observing or reasoning about raw worker output by adding explicit prohibitions to the Foreman role file and an explicit CHECKPOINT gate to the polaris-run chain.

## Context

In the [POL-283](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-283/implement-complete-cognition-librarian-lifecycle-wiring-and-adaptive) run, the Foreman consumed excessive tokens by observing and narrating worker activity (F1), and performed live intervention and repair (F4). Both failures are prompt/instruction failures — the current role file does not explicitly prohibit these behaviors.

Source: [POL-288](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-288/analyze-pol-283-foremanworker-behavioral-drift-and-runtime-boundary) postmortem `smartdocs/raw/pol-288-foreman-worker-drift-postmortem.md`, sections 4 and 8.
Parent implementation cluster: [POL-289](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-289/implement-foremanworker-boundary-hardening-from-pol-283-postmortem).

## Goal

Add explicit, unambiguous prohibitions to the Foreman role file and polaris-run chain so that a model following these instructions cannot rationalize observing worker output or performing live repair.

## Scope

* `.polaris/roles/foreman.md`
* `.polaris/skills/polaris-run/chain.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `foreman.md` contains: "The Foreman must not read, summarize, or reason about raw worker output. Only the CompactReturn JSON is valid Foreman input at CHECKPOINT."
- [ ] `foreman.md` explicitly prohibits live repair: "Repair of worker output is prohibited. Only abort-and-redispatch or emergency-takeover (with user approval) is allowed."
- [ ] `polaris-run/chain.md` has a CHECKPOINT gate step: "Discard all worker output except the CompactReturn JSON. Confirm only: status, commit, next_recommended_action."
- [ ] `chain.md` explicitly states: "Worker transcripts must not merge back into parent context."
- [ ] Existing chain step order is preserved; gate is inserted at the CHECKPOINT boundary

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

First child. No dependencies on sibling issues. Can be done before or alongside POL-290 (T1-2).

## Non-goals

* Do not add runtime enforcement (code changes) in this issue — instruction-only fix
* Do not change the dispatch or continue CLI commands
* Do not alter worker role files (that is POL-290)

POL-295.title: IMPLEMENT: Foreman/Worker boundary enforcement — POL-283 postmortem hardening
